textPlain: Kind of weird that NOP actually slows down the pipeline, as I'd think that would be the easiest thing to optimize out of the pipeline, unless instruction fetch is one of the main limiting factors.  Is it architecturally defined that NOP will slow down execution?

For the Commodore 64 there was a product called the C64 Snail which could slow it down.Later on the early PCs we had a Turbo Button, but since everyone had it in Turbo mode all the time it essentially was a way to slow down the machine.
Interesting.I had the impression, the turbo button was created to slow down new PCs, so they could run old software that relied heavily on CPU speed.
